3.question: In feminist SF, monstrous, hybrid bodies often serve to:

options.B: Function as metaphors questioning power and normativity

options.A: How power manages bodies, reproduction, and identity

options.B: A fragmented identity shaped by networks and code
options.D: A purely biological understanding of life

options.B: Entanglements among humans, technology, and environments
options.C: The separation of body and machine

9.question: The “future body” imagined in feminist technoculture is:

options.B: Hybrid, relational, and still politically contested
